title: Business Analyst-AI & Analytics
description:
Why Mizuho

At Mizuho, we provide the stability of an international industry leader with the career trajectory of a growing business. Our steady, strategic growth gives our people at all levels rewarding degrees of responsibility and richer work experience than a boutique firm or an established giant could offer alone.




It’s the local expertise of our employees that makes our global network so powerful. By collaborating with colleagues and clients who have the same ambition and drive, you can amplify your sphere of influence and base of knowledge as part of one of the largest—and growing—banks in the world.




Role Summary:




The AI & Analytics team is seeking an experienced Business Analyst to play a critical role in shaping, governing, and delivering enterprise‑scale AI, analytics, and intelligent automation initiatives across the bank. This role requires a strong blend of business analysis expertise, financial services domain knowledge, and the ability to operate independently with senior stakeholders.

The Business Analyst will lead complex requirements efforts, influence solution design, support AI governance and risk processes, and ensure analytics and AI solutions deliver measurable business value. The role is expected to demonstrate strong judgment, ownership, and leadership, while serving as a trusted partner to front‑office, risk, compliance, operations, and technology teams.




Key Responsibilities:




Business Analysis & Solution Definition

Lead end‑to‑end business analysis for complex AI, analytics, and intelligent automation initiatives, from problem definition through delivery and adoption.
Own the translation of business strategy and operating needs into clear use cases, functional requirements, user stories, and success metrics.
Partner closely with AI engineers, data engineers, and analytics leads to shape solution approaches and ensure alignment with business intent.
Challenge assumptions and proactively recommend alternative approaches where appropriate.

Senior Stakeholder Engagement

Act as a primary point of contact for senior business and technology stakeholders across multiple lines of business.
Facilitate working sessions, design reviews, and decision forums with executive‑level participants.
Provide clear, concise communication on scope, risks, trade‑offs, and delivery status, tailored to senior audiences.

Governance, Risk & Regulatory Alignment

Play a key role in AI and analytics governance, including model intake, approvals, documentation, and ongoing monitoring.
Ensure solutions comply with internal policies, model risk management standards, data governance requirements, and applicable regulatory expectations.
Contribute to materials for governance committees, audits, regulatory exams, and internal reviews.
Act as a control‑minded partner, identifying and escalating risks early and pragmatically.

Delivery Leadership & Execution

Provide analytical leadership across one or more workstreams within large or strategically important initiatives.
Support planning, dependency management, issue resolution, and delivery tracking in partnership with program and delivery leads.
Oversee user acceptance testing (UAT), validation of business outcomes, and readiness for production deployment.
Mentor junior analysts and provide informal guidance on analysis standards and best practices.

Business Value & Insight Generation

Analyze business processes, operational metrics, and performance data to identify opportunities for AI‑driven improvement.
Support development of business cases, value realization frameworks, and benefits tracking for AI and analytics initiatives.
Prepare executive‑ready presentations, summaries, and written materials for senior management and governance forums.




Required Experience

6–10 years of experience as a Business Analyst or similar role within financial services, investment banking, or capital markets.
Proven experience leading analysis for data, analytics, or AI‑enabled initiatives of meaningful scale and complexity.
Demonstrated experience operating in highly regulated environments with strong governance and control requirements.




Required Skills:




Advanced requirements elicitation, documentation, and stakeholder management skills.
Strong understanding of data, analytics, and AI concepts, including data flows, model lifecycles, and analytics delivery practices.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (PowerPoint, Excel); experience with Agile delivery tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence) preferred.
Excellent executive‑level written and verbal communication skills.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and initiatives concurrently.




Key Competencies:




Strategic & Analytical Thinking: Ability to connect analytics initiatives to broader business strategy and outcomes.
Leadership & Ownership: Takes accountability for results and operates with minimal oversight.
Stakeholder Influence: Trusted advisor capable of influencing senior business and technology leaders.
Risk Awareness & Judgment: Demonstrates sound judgment in balancing innovation, control, and regulatory considerations.




Qualifications:




Bachelor’s degree in business, Finance, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or a related field.
Advanced degree (MBA, MS) and/or relevant certifications (CBAP, PMI‑PBA, Agile BA) preferred.




Organization Overview:




Mizuho Global Services (MGS), Pune is an integral part of Mizuho Financial Group, one of the world’s leading financial institutions with a strong global presence across the Americas, EMEA, and Asia. Based in India, MGS Pune supports Mizuho’s international businesses by delivering high-quality, scalable, and resilient services across multiple functions.




MGS Pune plays a critical role in driving operational excellence, standardization, and innovation for Mizuho Americas. By combining deep domain expertise with strong process, technology, and analytical capabilities, it partners closely with regional and global teams to support corporate and investment banking, capital markets, and corporate services functions, while adhering to the highest standards of risk management, regulatory compliance, and control.




MGS Pune offers competitive compensation and benefits package aligned with industry standards and local market practices.




MGS Pune is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace.




Employment is subject to applicable background verification checks in accordance with Indian laws and company policies.

description:
Job Title: Business Analyst (BPMN / DMN)

Company: Blue Polaris

Location: Pune, India (Hybrid)

Job Type: Full-Time

Experience Level: 5 - 7Years




About Blue Polaris

Blue Polaris is a forward-thinking technology company focused on delivering cutting-edge automation and business solutions to global clients. We are committed to innovation, collaboration, and empowering our team to build efficient, intelligent systems that drive impactful business outcomes.

We’re currently looking for a Mid-level IBM BAW Developer to join our team and help shape the future of digital process automation.




Position Overview

We are looking for skilled Business Process Modelers / Business Analysts with hands-on experience in BPMN and/or DMN.

In this role, you will work on analyzing, designing, and improving business processes and decision logic using standard modeling techniques. Senior candidates with strong expertise and experience in leading process improvement initiatives are encouraged to apply.




Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain business process models using BPMN
Design and implement decision models and business rules using DMN
Work closely with stakeholders and teams to gather and validate requirements
Perform gap analysis and identify process improvement opportunities
Recommend efficient and scalable solutions
Ensure models follow organizational standards and quality guidelines
Support automation and digital transformation initiatives
Participate in workshops and present solutions to stakeholders
Mentor junior team members (for senior roles)




Required Skills & Qualifications

Hands-on experience in BPMN and/or DMN modeling
Strong understanding of business analysis and process improvement (Lean, Six Sigma is a plus)
Good analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
Ability to work independently and in a team environment
Good written and spoken English




Experience

Open to candidates with relevant experience
5+ years of experience preferred for senior roles

title: Business Analyst
description:
Role Overview

We are seeking a Business Analyst to join our product engineering team. This role is embedded within technology development and works closely with Engineering, Product Management, QA, and Operations to design, refine, and deliver scalable workflow-driven solutions.

The ideal candidate brings strong analytical depth, technical credibility, and hands-on experience working within complex, rule-based SaaS systems.

Core Responsibilities


Translate business and operational needs into clear, implementable user stories and acceptance criteria
Model end-to-end workflows, including exception handling and state transitions
Collaborate closely with Engineering during design and build cycles
Validate functionality using data and system behavior
Support production triage and root cause analysis
Drive clarity in ambiguous problem spaces and challenge incomplete requirements
Mentor junior BAs where applicable


Mandatory Skills & Experience

Technical & Analytical


Hands-on experience writing and executing SQL queries for data validation and analysis
Strong understanding of system workflows and backend data flows
Ability to independently analyze production issues and support root cause investigation
Experience working within Agile product development teams


Product & Workflow Design


Experience collaborating on UI designs using Figma or similar tools
Ability to create detailed process maps, user journeys, and workflow documentation
Strong experience defining business rules and configuration logic


Delivery & Ownership


Writes clear, testable user stories with structured acceptance criteria
Breaks down epics into implementable increments
Participates actively in backlog refinement, sprint planning, and demos
Drives UAT coordination and validation
3+ years of Business Analyst experience in product or technology development environments
Experience working in SaaS or enterprise workflow-driven systems


Healthcare Experience Required


2+ years of experience in US Healthcare technology, preferably within claims processing, submissions workflows, eligibility, or revenue cycle systems
Strong understanding of the US claim lifecycle, including submission flows, payer interactions, rejections, and exception handling
Experience working with systems that support payer-facing workflows, clearinghouse interactions, or regulated healthcare transaction processing
Familiarity with US healthcare operational and compliance considerations impacting submissions and workflow design

Experience documenting integration flows or data mappings
Experience in healthcare technology or regulated industries

Experience with reporting or dashboard tools
Exposure to operational KPI definition and performance metrics
Experience mentoring or leading other analysts
Familiarity with tools such as Postman, Azure DevOps, JIRA, Confluence

Veradigm believes in empowering our associates with the tools and flexibility to bring the best version of themselves to work. Through our generous benefits package with an emphasis on work/life balance, we give our employees the opportunity to allow their careers to flourish.

title: Jr Estimation Engineer
description:
Junior Estimation Contract Engineer

Location: Head Office

Job Summary

As a Junior Estimation Contract Engineer, I assist in preparing accurate cost estimates, quantity take-offs, and budget analysis for residential and commercial construction projects. I analyze drawings, specifications, and project requirements to support cost planning, procurement, and project execution while ensuring compliance with company standards and cost efficiency.

Key Responsibilities


Prepare quantity take-offs from architectural and structural drawings.
Develop rate analysis and detailed cost estimates for construction activities such as brickwork (BBM), plastering, gypsum work, painting, etc.
Coordinate with site and design teams for drawing revisions and updated BOQs.
Prepare and maintain Bar Bending Schedules (BBS) for structural elements.
Compare consultant BOQs with site data and identify discrepancies (e.g., plumbing works).
Generate work orders for contractors in Highrise and StockUp ERP systems after management approval of MOUs.
Maintain organized project documentation and Excel-based estimation sheets based on GFC drawings.
Assist senior engineers in cost estimation reports and budget tracking.
Track and update material requirements across projects, ensuring proper documentation and approvals in ERP systems.
Prepare accurate tiling quantity estimates, including wastage calculations, floor-wise and building-wise based on GFC drawings.


Skills


Strong analytical and numerical abilities
Proficiency in MS Excel (advanced formulas preferred)
Working knowledge of AutoCAD
Experience with ERP systems (Highrise, StockUp)
Attention to detail and documentation management
Good coordination and communication skills


Qualifications


Bachelor’s Degree / Diploma in Civil Engineering or a related field
Basic knowledge of construction methods, materials, and estimation techniques
Ability to read and interpret architectural and structural drawings
Understanding of BOQ (Bill of Quantities), rate analysis, and cost estimation
Familiarity with Bar Bending Schedule (BBS) preparation
Proficiency in MS Excel, AutoCAD, and basic ERP systems (e.g., Highrise, StockUp)
Knowledge of GFC drawings and quantity take-off methods
Strong analytical, numerical, and problem-solving skills
Good communication and coordination abilities

title: Advanced Analytics-Supply Chain
description:
Summary

Position Summary

Enterprise Performance

Our Enterprise Performance team is at the forefront of enterprise technology, working across finance, supply chain, and IT operations to support the delivery of holistic performance improvement and digital transformation. We support Deloitte client service teams of strategic advisors and architects, differentiated by our industry depth to help collaborate with leading insights providers and leverage your experience in strategy, process design, technology enablement, and operational services to enable the heart of business solutions.

Position Summary

Level: Advance analytics Consultant/Senior Consultant

As an experienced Consultant/ Senior Consultant at Deloitte Consulting, you will be responsible for individually delivering high quality work products within due timelines. Need-basis you will be mentoring and/or directing junior team members/liaising with onsite/offshore teams to understand the functional requirements.

Work you’ll do :

As a practitioner in Deloitte’s Supply Chain & Network Operations team, you will:


Collaborate with analytics teams to manage and analyze big and small data.
Apply advanced analytics and create impactful visualizations (Tableau, Qlik, PowerBI).
Deliver high-quality work and adapt quickly, independently or in teams.
Use strong SQL skills to wrangle and transform data across databases (MS SQL, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, MongoDB).
Employ data wrangling and analytics expertise using Python, R, SPSS, Minitab, and Excel.
Support teams by identifying and resolving technical or data issues, ensuring insights address business needs.


The T eam:

From our centers, we work with Deloitte consultants to analyze, design, develop, and build solutions to help clients reimagine, reshape and rewire the competitive fabric of their supply chains across industries. Our centers house a multitude of specialists, ranging from systems designers, architects, integrators, analytics experts, domain specialists to creative digital experts as well as cyber risk and human capital professionals. All work together on diverse projects from advisory services, advanced preconfigured solutions and methodologies, to brand building and campaign management. We are a unique blend of skills and experiences, yet we underline the value of each individual, providing customized career paths, fostering innovation and knowledge development with a focus on quality.

Qualifications :

Must have Skills :


3–10 years of experience in data visualization troubleshooting, including resolving object failures, database connectivity issues, and data processing errors, preferably within Tableau.
3+ years supporting data analytics using statistical and analytical software such as R, SAS, SPSS Statistics, SPSS Modeler, Minitab, or Matlab.
Experience in Supply Chain analytics, with familiarity in working with supply chain-related data sources such as SAP Planning or Oracle Planning modules.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with demonstrated ability to troubleshoot complex technical data analytics issues.
Proficiency in programming with Python or similar scripting languages (e.g., R, SQL).
Experience with ETL tools such as SSIS, Informatica, or Alteryx for data extraction, transformation, and loading.
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau, QlikView, Qlik Sense, or Power BI, enabling effective communication of insights.
Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, including Visio, PowerPoint, Excel, and Access, for documentation and presentation.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, enabling effective teamwork and stakeholder engagement across cross-functional teams.


Good to have Skills:


Ability to identify and resolve technical issues swiftly, supporting smooth analytics operations.
Strong problem solving and troubleshooting skills, with the capacity to exercise mature judgment in ambiguous situations.
Experience with big consulting firms or relevant industry experience, providing valuable context and best practices.
Exposure to advanced analytics methodologies or emerging technologies in data science.
Familiarity with additional supply chain platforms or planning modules beyond SAP and Oracle.
Experience with workflow automation or cloud-based analytics environments.
Professional certifications in analytics, data visualization, or supply chain management.


Education:


B.E, B.Tech, BBA from a leading school in India or abroad


Location:


Bengaluru/Hyderabad /Chennai/Pune

title: Junior Software Engineer
description:
Your Role

“..start your journey as a thoughtful software engineer today and grow into an AI‑capable developer of tomorrow…”

As a Junior Software Engineer, you will help implement backend services and integration components that support our enterprise applications. Working closely with Senior Engineers and Staff Engineers you’ll assist in shaping solutions, participating in design reviews, and learning best‑practice engineering standards.

You’ll collaborate with experienced front‑end and back‑end developers, contributing to complete features rather than isolated tasks as you gradually take on more responsibility. This role gives you hands-on experience with end‑to‑end product development and encourages you to build strong engineering fundamentals.

Operating in a multinational environment, you’ll gain exposure to diverse stakeholders and systems where reliability, maintainability, and long‑term quality are essential. You will also have opportunities to explore practical applications of GenAI that enhance developer productivity and support broader business efficiency.

What You Will Work On


Contributing to backend services and APIs for core enterprise systems (e.g., product information management, ERP and e‑commerce integrations, workflow automations)
Assisting in developing event‑driven integrations across platforms

title: Analyst/Senior Analyst (TMT)
description:
Location

New Delhi

Job Description

Job title: Analyst/Senior Analyst (TMT)

Company Description

We’re L.E.K. Consulting, a global strategy consultancy working with business leaders to seize competitive advantage and amplify growth. Our insights are catalysts that reshape the trajectory of our clients’ businesses, uncovering opportunities and empowering them to master their moments of truth. Since 1983, our worldwide practice—spanning the Americas, Asia Pacific, and Europe—has guided leaders across all industries, from global corporations to emerging entrepreneurial businesses and private equity investors. Looking for more? Visit

Position Overview

The L.E.K. Capability Network (LCN) is an integrated part of L.E.K. Consulting, collaborating closely with consulting teams in L.E.K.’s worldwide network of offices to support them on their toughest and most impactful assignments.

The Analyst / Senior Analyst at LCN plays a critical role in supporting case teams by leading analytical modules, managing research initiatives, and providing insightful recommendations.

A typical project might involve an in-depth primary research campaign to identify and triage potential experts to speak to in a particular field, before holding discussions with them to discover their insights on a topic. It could involve rigorous secondary research to prove or disprove a client hypothesis, leveraging the full arsenal of L.E.K. toolkits and databases. Or it could mean undertaking a critical analysis to illuminate a pattern from the noise around it, to substantiate a strategic decision. They are expected to take greater ownership of their work, proactively structure analysis, and effectively communicate findings. This role requires strong problem-solving skills, the ability to manage multiple tasks across time zones, and a collaborative mindset to mentor junior analysts and contribute to L.E.K.'s culture. More information can be found at

We are currently hiring for Analyst/Senior Analysts into the LCN, a role which will report into the LCN Team lead/Team manager

Primary Research

Responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:


Manage interview campaign including creating IV guide, expert identification, managing expert networks and other vendors, and manage real-time update
Independently work with consulting staff on projects by sourcing content experts and key opinion leaders (e.g., with LinkedIn, expert networks, cold calling, etc.)
Write solicitations for experts to conduct interviews with case teams, and custom solicitations for key opinion leaders (e.g., referring to papers he / she has published)
Conduct local / international interview campaigns and summarize key takeaways from the interview to help case teams derive strategic conclusions
Extract and curate key insights from interviews to validate or challenge case hypotheses, driving informed decision-making


Secondary research


Own end-to-end secondary research modules, identifying relevant data sources and extracting key insights
Collaborate with teams to identify secondary research resources that address client areas of interest (e.g., journal articles, marketing / industry reports, government publications)
Confirm/disprove case team hypotheses, deriving insights and patterns from unstructured problem statements using publicly available reports, articles, thought pieces, company filings and GenAI
Independently contribute to case teams by applying structured problem-solving techniques to test and validate case hypotheses


Analysis: Modelling / surveys


Manage and deliver end-to-end survey analysis including coding, testing, analysis and curating insights
Co-develop comprehensive models such as market models, revenue models, and cost models to support various due-diligence cases
Utilize Excel, Alteryx, Tableau, and other analytical tools to structure data and create compelling visualizations
Review client data to understand gaps in datasets


Slide preparation and quality control


Design and recommend analytical frameworks to effectively present insights in a clear, accurate, and impactful manner
Develop clear and compelling commentary that extracts actionable insights
Create sophisticated visuals in PowerPoint and Tableau for consulting teams to use in client presentations
Collaborate with L.E.K. Graphic Design team to recommend visually interesting layouts for showing complex data
Review slide formatting for quality control, making layouts consistent and in line with L.E.K. brand standards (e.g., updating L.E.K. IP to current visual standards)


Coaching and mentorship


Provide support and oversight to junior analysts on projects, ensuring they meet project objectives
Coach junior analysts on technical skillsets such as secondary research using databases, effective slide writing and data analysis
Share industry insights and best practices with team members to enhance their skills and knowledge.


Case closure support


Prepare redacted versions of materials for delivery to KM
Prepare PowerPoint release documents for case team to deliver to client
Facilitate access agreements process for teams interacting with investors (e.g., as part of a due diligence)


Qualifications


The ideal candidate will have 3-5 years of relevant experience with a top-tier consulting firm
An undergraduate degree is required
Fair understanding of business concepts and their practical applications
Ability to work on multiple tasks and activities at once and flexibility to re-prioritize
Expert PowerPoint and Excel skills
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
High attention to detail


Benefits And Perks


L.E.K. offers you the opportunity to work in a hybrid setup
We are among the best paying firms globally
Generous time away
We have Parental leaves (Maternity and Paternity) and flexible work options for new mothers
We provide childcare facilities in Mumbai (Day care / Creche services).
Wellness benefits – Health Insurance, Wellness Apps, Gym reimbursement, etc.


Show more

title: Junior Engineer
description:
Amex GBT is a place where colleagues find inspiration in travel as a force for good and – through their work – can make an impact on our industry. We’re here to help our colleagues achieve success and offer an inclusive and collaborative culture where your voice is valued.

What You’ll Do


Troubleshooting skills with Log analysis, Tracing, Heap dump analysis, Performance issues, and debugging.
Docker, GitHub, Jenkins – create basis pipelines, understanding of Platform as a Service (PaaS) for application hosting.
Knowledge of XML, JSON, YAML, XSD, WSDL
Low-level design documentation – Class, sequence diagrams
Understanding of Static code analysis tools like Sonar, Conducting code reviews.
Demonstrated willingness to learn new technologies and takes pride in how fast they develop working software



What We’re Looking For


Bachelor’s degree in a computer science, information technology, with 1-2yrs of experience in Java, Spring boot, J2EE
Must have an understanding of modern Java (JDK 17/21/25+) features such as virtual threads and pattern matching, proficiency in the Spring Boot ecosystem, and exposure to building cloud-native microservices with Docker and Kubernetes.
Must have knowledge of building serverless applications using TypeScript via the AWS Cloud Development Kit (CDK) and the AWS SDK, with exposure to deploying full-stack solutions through AWS Lambda, Amazon DynamoDB, and AWS Amplify.
Exposure to Oracle/DB2/PostgreSQL/Stored procedure/SQL development.
Primary Skills:
Must be willing and able to work in 2nd shifts (3:00 PM IST to 12:00 AM IST) and occasional weekends as required.
The candidate needs to work in App Support project
Must be willing to work from Bangalore office location minimum 3 days in a week.
Exposure to object-oriented design and coding skills across a variety of platforms Java/J2EE, SOA/Web Services, Spring Boot framework, REST API, JSON, HTML/HTML5, CSS3, XML, AJAX, jQuery, JavaScript, AngularJS, Selenium, Python and RabbitMQ.



Location

Bangalore, India

The #TeamGBT Experience

Work and life: Find your happy medium at Amex GBT.


Flexible benefits are tailored to each country and start the day you do. These include health and welfare insurance plans, retirement programs, parental leave, adoption assistance, and wellbeing resources to support you and your immediate family.
Travel perks: get a choice of deals each week from major travel providers on everything from flights to hotels to cruises and car rentals.
Develop the skills you want when the time is right for you, with access to over 20,000 courses on our learning platform, leadership courses, and new job openings available to internal candidates first.
We strive to champion Inclusion in every aspect of our business at Amex GBT. You can connect with colleagues through our global INclusion Groups, centered around common identities or initiatives, to discuss challenges, obstacles, achievements, and drive company awareness and action.
And much more!



All applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to age, sex, gender (and characteristics related to sex and gender), pregnancy (and related medical conditions), race, color, citizenship, religion, disability, or any other class or characteristic protected by law.

Click Here for Additional Disclosures in Accordance with the LA County Fair Chance Ordinance.

Furthermore, we are committed to providing reasonable accommodation to qualified individuals with disabilities. Please let your recruiter know if you need an accommodation at any point during the hiring process. For details regarding how we protect your data, please consult the Amex GBT Recruitment Privacy Statement.

What if I don’t meet every requirement? If you’re passionate about our mission and believe you’d be a phenomenal addition to our team, don’t worry about “checking every box;" please apply anyway. You may be exactly the person we’re looking for!

title: Assistant Manager - Copy
description:
Ways of Working - Remote - Employees will have the freedom to work remotely all through the year. These employees, who form a large majority, will come together in their base location (Bangalore) for a week, once every quarter.




About Swiggy

Swiggy is India’s leading on-demand delivery platform with a tech-first approach to logistics and a solution-first approach to consumer demands. With a presence in 500+ cities across India, partnerships with hundreds of thousands of restaurants, an employee base of over 5000, and a 2 lakh+ strong independent fleet of Delivery Executives, we deliver unparalleled convenience driven by continuous innovation. Built on the back of robust ML technology and fueled by terabytes of data processed every day, Swiggy offers a fast, seamless, and reliable delivery experience for millions of customers across India. From starting out as a hyperlocal food delivery service in 2014 to becoming India’s leading on-demand convenience platform today, our capabilities result not only in lightning-fast delivery for customers but also in a productive and fulfilling experience for our employees.




Roles & Responsibilities

Write clear, compelling, and engaging copy for multiple channels including brand films, performance marketing, social media, in-app channels and delivery partner communications, ensuring alignment with Swiggy’s tone and voice.
Develop innovative and creative concepts and messaging that resonate with the delivery partner audience and reflect campaign objectives.
Collaborate closely with the creative team, designers, and marketers to produce integrated content that enhances brand storytelling and user engagement.
Edit, proofread, and elevate copy produced by junior writers to maintain high editorial standards and consistency.
Translate marketing briefs into powerful written communication that inspires and motivates delivery partners.
Maintain and evolve a consistent brand voice specifically tailored to delivery partners across all communications.
Use insights and feedback to refine copy for improved impact and effectiveness.
Keep abreast of industry trends and best practices to inject fresh ideas into the marketing strategy.
Contribute hands-on to content creation while mentoring junior copywriters to grow their skills and craft.




Desired Skills & Experience

Experience as a copywriter, preferably with exposure to brand marketing, and specially social media marketing or advertising agency environments.
Strong portfolio showcasing a range of creative work across diverse platforms and formats, with emphasis on user-focused, result-driven writing.
Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills with impeccable attention to detail.
Ability to convey complex messages in simple, relatable language that speaks directly to delivery partners.
Aptitude for collaborating with design and marketing teams to create cohesive campaign assets.
Understanding of digital marketing dynamics, including social, performance, and brand communications.
Proactive attitude with the ability to work independently and manage multiple deadlines while maintaining quality.
A mindset that values creativity as an evolving process defined by audience connection, clarity, and impact.




We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Junior Full Stack Developer
description:
Job Description – Junior Full Stack Developer

Department: Technology & Digital / Global Business Solutions

Location: Bangalore, India

Reports To: Janes Beets, Application Manager, IT

Job Purpose

The Junior Full Stack Developer contributes to the design, development, and maintenance of internal business applications used across Epiroc. The role supports product teams in building secure, scalable, and user‑friendly digital solutions for sourcing applications and collaborates closely with senior developers to ensure high‑quality delivery.

Key Responsibilities

Software Development


Develop and maintain web applications across the full stack using modern frameworks and tools.
Implement front‑end features using build tooling such as Vite, Webpack, Angular CLI, or React build pipelines (proficiency in at least one required).
Build and maintain ASP.NET / C# back‑end services and REST APIs.


Security & Identity


Integrate secure authentication and authorization using Azure AD / SSO (OAuth2/OIDC, tokens, scopes, consent).


Cloud & DevOps


Contribute to application and infrastructure automation using Azure and Infrastructure‑as‑Code practices.


Agile Collaboration


Participate in Jira Sprints, daily standups, sprint planning, and other Agile ceremonies.
Work proactively, manage time effectively, and keep stakeholders informed.


Teamwork & Learning


Collaborate through pair‑programming and code reviews.
Seek guidance from Senior Developers and remain open to continuous learning and feedback.


Experience

Required Qualifications


2–3 years of hands‑on software development in a full‑stack or front‑end focused role.


Technical Skills


Frontend build tooling: Vite, Webpack, Angular CLI, React (at least one required; familiarity with several is a plus).
Back‑end: ASP.NET / C#, REST API development.
Authentication & Authorization: Azure AD / SSO, OAuth2/OIDC.
Exposure to Azure and Infrastructure‑as‑Code concepts.
Knowledge of API & EDI Integrations is advantageous


Methodology & Tools


Working knowledge of Jira, Agile/Scrum ways of working.


Soft Skills


Strong communication and stakeholder‑management skills.

title: Data Analyst
description:
The Role

Reporting to the Global Manager, Data Access Control, the Data Access Control Analyst is the driver of all technical aspects of the Data Governance team. The Data Access Analyst will apply all rules and associated policies and procedures of the Data Governance process to all user and data access requests. The Data Access Analyst should be customer focused and impacts organisational performance on processes related to client data. This individual should apply their technical skills to execute the Data Governance framework and associated policies and procedures. The individual will also be building ETL pipelines and developing dashboards for auditing and analytical purposes to ensure our data remains governed.




Key Responsibilities

Data Engineering & ETL: Build and automate robust data pipelines in Domo using Magic ETL and Python. You’ll integrate and consolidate metadata from multiple instances to ensure high-quality data accessibility for reporting and auditing..
Advanced Visualization: Design, build, and maintain sophisticated dashboards in Domo, Power BI, or Tableau that provide "at-a-glance" clarity for executive stakeholders.
Data Governance: Act as a steward for data integrity. You’ll implement governance standards to ensure data consistency, security, and accuracy across the organization.
Strategic Analysis: Perform deep-dive investigations into trends and variances, turning raw numbers into narratives that support our long-term strategic initiatives.
Modernization & Mentorship: Lead the shift toward automated reporting. You’ll educate junior team members on new tools and best practices in Python and BI.

What You Bring

Technical Excellence: Strong proficiency in Python (Pandas, NumPy) and advanced SQL.
BI Mastery: Extensive experience with at least one major BI tool (Domo, Power BI, or Tableau).
Analytical Mindset: You can solve complex problems independently by critical thinking and are comfortable navigating "grey areas" where precedents might not exist.
Governance Exposure: A solid understanding of data lineage, metadata management, and data quality frameworks.
Data Quality:Monitor and track data quality issues, identify gaps, and apply action plans to remediate gap
Communication: The ability to explain complex technical insights to non-technical audiences and influence decision-making.

title: Analyst/Associate Consultant - Corporate Treasury
description:
Financial Services Risk Management, Corporate Treasury Advisory – Analyst or ACon




About the Company




KPMG is a global network of professional firms providing Audit, Tax and Advisory services. We operate in 156 countries and have 152,000 people working in member firms around the world. KPMG in India, a professional services firm, is the Indian member firm of KPMG International and was established in September 1993. Our professionals leverage the global network of firms, providing detailed knowledge of local laws, regulations, markets and competition. KPMG in India provide services to over 4,500 international and national clients, in India. KPMG has offices across India in Delhi, Chandigarh, Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Pune, Chennai, Bangalore, Kochi, Hyderabad and Kolkata. The Indian firm has access to more than 7,000 Indian and expatriate professionals, many of whom are internationally trained. We strive to provide rapid, performance-based, industry-focused and technology-enabled services, which reflect a shared knowledge of global and local industries and our experience of the Indian business environment. KPMG Advisory professionals provide advice and assistance to enable companies, intermediaries and public sector bodies to mitigate risk, improve performance, and create value. KPMG firms provide a wide range of Risk Consulting, Management Consulting and Transactions & Restructuring services that can help clients respond to immediate needs as well as put in place the strategies for the longer term. With increasing regulatory requirements, the need for greater transparency in operations, and disclosure norms, stakeholders require assurance beyond the traditional critique of numbers. Hence assurance is being increasingly required on industry issues, business risks and key business processes. The Governance, Risk & Compliance Services practice assists companies and public sector bodies to mitigate risk, improve performance and create value. We assist our clients to effectively manage business and process risks by providing a full spectrum of corporate governance, risk management, and Compliance Services. These services are tailored to meet client’s individual needs, and provide effective support to management in meeting the challenges and opportunities presented by today's complex business environment. Our professionals provide the experience to help companies stay on track and deal with risks that could unhinge their business survival. Our services enable clients to effectively co-ordinate their key growth, quality and operational challenges and working in partnership with us, clients have the benefits of KPMG's experienced, objective, and industry-grounded viewpoints.




Key skills and qualifications

Bachelor degree like Bachelor in Business Administration/ Bachelor in Financial Accounting/ Bachelor in Banking and Insurance from a Tier 1 college/ institute along with FRM (both level cleared) in first attempt and/ or Chartered Financial Analyst from GARP
Strong academic record at 10, 10+2 and graduate examinations
Good understanding of corporate treasury operations and financial products
Understanding of Spot and Derivative market operations for interest rate, foreign exchange products including risk management (high level hedging strategies) and valuation aspects of the same
Well-versed in MS Excel, MS PowerPoint and Power BI. Should be able to build MS Excel based models for risk quantifications using various methods.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Leadership experience at school/college level preferable
High levels of commitment to work




Role and responsibilities

The candidate will be expected to assist assignments involving assisting Indian and international corporate organizations in their treasury operations
The candidate will be expected to assist Assistant Managers to Senior Managers in Corporate Treasury assignments including advisory / Internal Audit/ review of treasury operations areas such as foreign exchange risk management, interest rate risk management, commodities risk management, cash and liquidity management, borrowings management, investments management.
The candidate should be well versed with concepts like Value at Risk and its computational methodologies, Expected shortfall method, Sensitivity analysis, etc. Candidate should understand key concepts of financial risk management.
The assignments would include providing advisory / conducting reviews for the areas mentioned above covering strategy, policy, governance, day to day operations, process improvement opportunities, compliance to internal directives and regulatory requirements etc.
The candidate will be expected to take-up business development initiatives and assisting in creating proposals, responses to RFPs, thought leaderships, etc.
The candidate will be expected to have very strong communication skills and confidence to interact independently with junior to mid-level client personnel.
The position is based out of Mumbai and the candidate must be open to travel




Compensation is competitive with industry standards. Details of the compensation breakup will be shared with short-listed candidates only.




Equal Opportunity Statement




The opportunity is now! If you are interested in being part of a dynamic team, serving clients and reaching your full potential – KPMG Advisory Services is for you!

title: Accountant
description:
The Company
PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.

We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.

We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.

Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.

Job Description Summary:
This job is responsible for timely and effective reconciliation of all assigned General Ledger accounts, ensuring accurate clearing of reconciling items per company policy. You’ll ensure accurate posting of entries in SAP and customer accounts, research and resolve outstanding reconciling items, and apply advanced techniques to identify their sources. Additionally, you’ll participate in month-end closing activities, manage day-to-day accounting tasks, and identify process improvement opportunities in reporting, reconciliation, and analysis.

Job Description:
Essential Responsibilities:


Responsible for timely and effective reconciliation of all assigned General Ledger accounts
Ensure accurate clearing of reconciling items in accordance with Company Policy
Ensure accurate posting of general ledger entries in SAP and customer accounts
Involved in research, track, and timely resolve outstanding reconciling items and related issues
Apply advanced research techniques to accurately identify the source of reconciling items
Participate in month-end close activities
Identify and recommend process improvement opportunities in reporting, reconciliation, and analysis
Communicate with internal resources and/or processors to escalate and resolve differences
Drive technology adoption and changes to improve efficiency
Assist with escalated cases



Minimum Qualifications:


1+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.



Additional Responsibilities And Preferred Qualifications
What you need to know about the role:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated Staff Accountant to join our Record-to-Report team. This role plays a critical part in supporting accurate financial reporting, maintaining strong internal controls, and ensuring timely month-end close activities. The ideal candidate is someone who combines a solid accounting foundation with a strong desire to learn, improve processes, and collaborate across teams.

Your Impact:

The Staff Accountant will be responsible for preparing journal entries, performing account reconciliations, supporting close and reporting activities, and assisting with audits. This role provides strong exposure to core accounting processes and is well-suited for candidates looking to grow their career within a dynamic, fast-paced organization.

Your Day-to-Day:


Prepare and post routine journal entries in accordance with company accounting policies and US GAAP
Perform account reconciliations, investigate variances, follow up on open or unusual items and monitor the aging
Perform data entry and transactional accounting tasks with a high level of accuracy
Support period-end close activities, including close checklists and deliverables
Assist in maintaining process documentation, desk procedures, and close checklists
Assist with preparation of financial reports, schedules, and variance analysis
Participate in testing and validation activities related to system enhancements or upgrades
Help ensure compliance with internal controls, SOX requirements, and accounting policies
Respond to routine inquiries from internal business partners
Support internal and external audits by preparing schedules and responding to audit requests



What You’ll Bring:


Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Accounting, Finance, or related discipline from an accredited university
1–3 years of relevant accounting experience, including internships, articleship, or entry-level roles
Strong understanding of basic accounting principles and financial statements
Hands-on experience supporting month-end close activities and account reconciliations
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including formulas, lookups, and basic data analysis
Strong attention to detail, documentation, and data accuracy
Ability to work effectively in a global, matrixed environment and across time zones



Preferred Qualifications:


CA (Chartered Accountant) – Inter or Final qualified, or CA Articleship experience preferred
Experience working with multinational companies or shared services
Exposure to ERP systems such as SAP (ECC or S/4HANA), Oracle, or similar finance systems
Familiarity with close management, reconciliation, or automation tools (e.g., BlackLine)
Basic understanding of internal controls, SOX compliance, and audit requirements
Strong written and verbal communication skills



PayPal does not charge candidates any fees for courses, applications, resume reviews, interviews, background checks, or onboarding. Any such request is a red flag and likely part of a scam. To learn more about how to identify and avoid recruitment fraud please visit https://careers.pypl.com/contact-us.

For the majority of employees, PayPal's balanced hybrid work model offers 3 days in the office for effective in-person collaboration and 2 days at your choice of either the PayPal office or your home workspace, ensuring that you equally have the benefits and conveniences of both locations.

Our Benefits:

At PayPal, we’re committed to building an equitable and inclusive global economy. And we can’t do this without our most important asset-you. That’s why we offer benefits to help you thrive in every stage of life. We champion your financial, physical, and mental health by offering valuable benefits and resources to help you care for the whole you.

We have great benefits including a flexible work environment, employee shares options, health and life insurance and more. To learn more about our benefits please visit https://www.paypalbenefits.com

Who We Are:

To learn more about our culture and community visit https://about.pypl.com/who-we-are/default.aspx

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

PayPal provides equal employment opportunity (EEO) to all persons regardless of age, color, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, race, religion, creed, gender, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, genetic information, marital status, status with regard to public assistance, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, PayPal will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you are unable to submit an application because of incompatible assistive technology or a disability, please contact us at paypalglobaltalentacquisition@paypal.com.

Belonging at PayPal:

Our employees are central to advancing our mission, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can do their best work with a sense of purpose and belonging. Belonging at PayPal means creating a workplace with a sense of acceptance and security where all employees feel included and valued. We are proud to have a diverse workforce reflective of the merchants, consumers, and communities that we serve, and we continue to take tangible actions to cultivate inclusivity and belonging at PayPal.

Any general requests for consideration of your skills, please Join our Talent Community.

We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates. Please don’t hesitate to apply.

title: Full-Stack Developer Internship
description:
Full-Stack Developer Internship


Location: Hybrid in Bengaluru


Employment Type: Internship (3-6 months)


Experience Level: Entry-Level/Recent Graduate





About Acowale


We're building Acodash - the world's first AI-powered Smart Operating System for Business. One simple dashboard replaces every digital tool a business needs, integrated with AI Assistants running businesses 24/7.


Why we're different:


AI-first approach (backed by Microsoft)

Simple as WhatsApp, powerful as SAP

Multi-location ready and affordable

Fast-growing product startup with LoI from 1.5M+ merchants


The Role


We're seeking a motivated Full-Stack Developer Intern to join our engineering team and contribute to building scalable systems on the AcoMono project. You'll work on both backend and frontend, learn from experienced engineers, and gain hands-on experience with modern technologies in a startup environment.


This is a unique opportunity to:


Work on real features used by thousands of merchants

Learn backend and frontend development best practices

Get mentored by experienced engineers

Contribute to a mission-critical platform serving millions

Experience startup culture and fast-paced development




Requirements



Backend Development


Implement backend features using TypeScript and Node.js under guidance

Write clean, maintainable code following team standards

Build and optimize PostgreSQL queries with senior engineer support

Contribute to API development and documentation

Write unit tests using Jest for your code


Frontend Development


Contribute to React components using TypeScript

Work with Vite build tooling and modern frontend practices

Implement features designed by the team

Ensure UI-backend integration works smoothly

Learn state management and data fetching patterns


Learning & Collaboration


Participate in code reviews and learn from feedback

Ask questions and seek mentorship from senior engineers

Attend technical discussions and architecture sessions

Contribute to improving documentation and code quality

Collaborate with team members on feature delivery


Requirements


Must Have:


Strong foundation in JavaScript or TypeScript

Basic understanding of React or similar frontend framework

Familiarity with SQL and relational databases

Git basics and version control understanding

Problem-solving mindset and willingness to learn

Good communication skills


Nice to Have:


Experience with Node.js or backend development

Knowledge of REST APIs and HTTP concepts

Exposure to Docker or containerization

Understanding of testing and test-driven development

Experience with Agile workflows

Personal projects on GitHub showcasing your work


Tech Stack


Backend: TypeScript, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Express or similar framework Frontend: React, TypeScript, Vite, CSS/Tailwind DevOps: Docker, GitHub Testing: Jest Architecture: Monorepo (pnpm workspaces), API-first design


What We're Looking For


You're curious and eager to learn cutting-edge technologies You write code you can explain and defend You ask good questions and aren't afraid to say you don't know You're collaborative and enjoy working with others You're excited about building products that impact millions You can work independently but also reach out for help when needed





Benefits



Why Join Us?


Learn modern tech stack in a real product environment Work on a mission-critical platform serving 1.5M+ merchants Get mentored by experienced engineers Experience how a startup operates Potential for full-time conversion based on performance Competitive internship stipend





What to Expect


You'll start with onboarding and smaller tasks to get familiar with the codebase Within 2-3 weeks, you'll start contributing to real features You'll have regular check-ins with your mentor to discuss progress and learnings Code reviews are part of the process - expect constructive feedback By the end of your internship, you should have shipped meaningful features We'll provide clear feedback on your performance and potential





Ready to Learn and Grow?


If you're passionate about full-stack development and want to learn from experienced engineers while building a product used by millions, we'd love to meet you.


Let's build something remarkable together. Start your journey with us.

description:

Sapiens International Corporation (NASDAQ and TASE: SPNS) is a leading global provider of software solutions for the insurance industry, with a growing presence in the financial services sector. Sapiens also services the workers’ compensation and financial and compliance markets. title: Early Careers Recruitment_Associate
description:
Job Description

Build strategic partnerships and a diverse entry-level talent pipeline as a Junior Recruiter in the Early Careers Recruitment team.

Are you ready to shape the future of talent acquisition at JPMorgan Chase? Join our dynamic Early Careers team and be at the forefront of recruiting top-tier junior talent.

As a Campus Recruiter within the Early Careers team, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of our workforce by building a robust pipeline of diverse intern talent across our programs. You will serve as the subject matter expert in early careers recruitment, leveraging your expertise to attract and engage top-tier candidates.

Job Responsibilities


Manages the recruiting process end-to-end and formulates an effective recruiting strategy through partnerships with business stakeholders and the broader Early Careers team.
Builds and owns key relationships with business leaders, program management, and other stakeholders to provide visibility on recruiting activity and collaborate on engagement plans.
Partners internally with other areas of the organization – including Talent Acquisition Marketing and Employer Brand to create a consistent and engaging candidate experience that promotes JPMorgan Chase as an employer of choice.
Owns and executes any diversity-specific initiatives, location-based on country talent acquisition strategy.
Builds and maintains relationships with universities and internal stakeholders and leads the design and delivery of an effective events plan for any in-person and/or virtual activity.
Partners closely with the Early Careers Operations team to facilitate a seamless candidate and business experience in line with all appropriate recruiting risk and controls.
Actively manages all candidates through the pipeline in a timely manner including candidate progression, candidate review, Superday management, offer communications, etc.
Plans and delivers the summer internship program end-to-end; including support and mentorship for interns, performance development, and conversion to the full-time analyst program.
Utilizes data and reporting to identify improvements to existing processes, provides key metrics to stakeholders with a focus on return and outcomes, and actively manages and tracks budgets. 


Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills


3 years plus of Human resources experience and MBA or similar qualification
Ability to work in a team in addition to working independently.
Strong organizational, planning, time-management skills, and the ability to multi-task.
Strategic thinker with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Project management experience: demonstrated capability to lead and manage projects.
Strong relationship management skills and the ability to interact with all levels of stakeholders.
Effective influencing skills and demonstrated ability to build internal and external relationships.
Proven attention to detail with excellent communication skills: verbally and written.
Flexible and adaptable to a fast-paced work environment.
Exceptional Microsoft Office skills (i.e., Word, PowerPoint, Excel).


Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills


Recruiting experience in volume/early career recruiting
Work experience within global Financial Services  
Experience with candidate sourcing platforms e.g. Handshake, LinkedIn etc.  


Additional Information

This role requires regular travel within India. Join us to make a significant impact on our talent acquisition strategy and help shape the future of our workforce.

About Us

JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world’s most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

About The Team

Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we’re setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success.

Human Resources plays a critical role in driving the employee experience, shaping the firm’s culture and building a diverse and inclusive workforce. We are a strategic partner to the business — working with leaders across the firm to hire, develop and retain the best people and help meet business goals. Together, we foster a work environment in which our people are supported, feel like they belong and are able to make an impact through their work. We develop and deliver a suite of products and services that help make JPMorgan Chase an employer of choice and drive our business forward.

Job Description Summary:
This job initiates accounting processes to ensure financial statements meet local GAAP and monitor the closing process, posting journal entries for necessary adjustments. You’ll meet statutory requirements for regulatory reports, external audits, and may participate in the preparation of financial statements. Additionally, you’ll support the organization in meeting local and global regulatory obligations, prepare annual reconciliations between local GAAP and IFRS, and drive process control rigor to support quarterly financial certifications.

Job Description:
Essential Responsibilities:


Initiate accounting processes to ensure financial statements meet local GAAP
Monitor the accounting closing process and post journal entries for any required adjustments in the local ledger
Support the organization in meeting local and global regulatory obligations
Drive process control rigor to support quarterly certification on financials
Implement monthly financial reports and analytics to support structured risk-based monthly financial reviews
Implement statutory financial statements and ensure timely completion of statutory audits
Facilitate month-end, quarter-end, and year-end accounting close activities
Review and prepare journal entries, account reconciliations, roll-forwards, and month-end reporting packages



Minimum Qualifications:


3+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.



Additional Responsibilities And Preferred Qualifications
What you need to know about the role:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated Senior Accountant to join our Record-to-Report team. This role plays a critical part in ensuring accurate financial reporting, maintaining strong internal controls, and ensuring timely month-end close activities. This role goes beyond execution and requires strong professional judgment, end-to-end process ownership, and the ability to operate independently while partnering cross-functionally.

Your Impact:

The Staff Accountant will be responsible for preparing journal entries, performing account reconciliations, supporting close and reporting activities, and assisting with audits. This role provides strong exposure to core accounting processes and is well-suited for candidates looking to grow their career within a dynamic, fast-paced organization.

Your Day-to-Day:


Execute and period-end close activities, such as prepare and review manual journal entries, perform account reconciliations and account fluctuation analysis
Own and manage end-to-end accounting processes, ensuring consistency, accuracy, and timelines of close
Apply professional judgment in accordance with US GAAP, internal accounting policies, and SOX requirements
Perform detailed variance (flux) analysis, identify root causes, and coordinate issue resolution with relevant stakeholders
Act as a primary point of contact for assigned processes, partnering with Global Controllership, FP&A, Tax, Treasury, and Operations teams
Review work performed by Staff Accountants, ensuring adherence to quality standards, and provide coaching, guidance, and informal mentorship
Drive process standardization, documentation, and continuous improvement across assigned processes
Identify and implement automation and efficiency opportunities to improve scalability and reduce manual effort
Perform testing and validation activities related to system enhancements or upgrades
Lead and coordinate responses for internal and external audit requests related to assigned processes



What You’ll Bring:


Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline from an accredited institution
5+ years of progressive accounting experience, preferably within a multinational environment
Strong working knowledge of US GAAP and global accounting close processes
Hands-on experience with supporting period-end close in an SLA-driven environment
Proficiency with large ERP systems (SAP ECC/S4 preferred) and related close management, reconciliation, or automation tools (e.g., BlackLine)
Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups, large data analysis)
Strong written and verbal communication skills



Nice to Have:


CA (India), CPA (US), ACCA, or equivalent professional qualification
Experience in technology, fintech, or payments industries
Prepare and post routine journal entries in accordance with company accounting policies and US GAAP
Perform account reconciliations, investigate variances, follow up on open or unusual items and monitor the aging
Perform data entry and transactional accounting tasks with a high level of accuracy
Support period-end close activities, including close checklists and deliverables
Assist in maintaining process documentation, desk procedures, and close checklists
Assist with preparation of financial reports, schedules, and variance analysis
Participate in testing and validation activities related to system enhancements or upgrades
Help ensure compliance with internal controls, SOX requirements, and accounting policies
Respond to routine inquiries from internal business partners
Support internal and external audits by preparing schedules and responding to audit requests



Preferred Qualifications:


CA (Chartered Accountant) – Inter or Final qualified, or CA Articleship experience preferred
Experience working with multinational companies or shared services / global capability centers (GCCs)
Exposure to ERP systems such as SAP (ECC or S/4HANA), Oracle, or similar finance systems
Familiarity with close management, reconciliation, or automation tools (e.g., BlackLine)
Basic understanding of internal controls, SOX compliance, and audit requirements
Strong written and verbal communication skills

title: Business Valuation Senior
description:
Why We Stand Out

Seeking a new challenge where your professional and personal aspirations are not only possible but supported? Kaufman Rossin might be just the place for you!

Kaufman Rossin Professional Services Private Limited's (the "Company") offices are located in the World Trade Center (WTC) in Bangalore, Karnataka, India, and at Udyog Vihar, in Gurgaon, Haryana, India.

The Bangalore office provides a range of services, including risk management, corporate governance, tax, assurance, and family office services. Out of the Gurgaon office, we render highly specialized back-office alternative investment services for global hedge funds and related fund types. As one of the top accounting firms in the US, our foundation is "people first". In the words of James Kaufman, one of our founders, "We prioritize our people, their development, and their well-being. Our values are translated into action every day."

Kaufman Rossin, headquartered in Miami, Florida has been celebrated as the Best Place to Work in South Florida more than a dozen time. The firm has grown to over 700 employees, with offices spanning the tri-county area and, sister entities, Kaufman Rossin Wealth and Kaufman Rossin Alternative Investment Services.

The Firm is ranked 49th among the top 100 firms in the US by Inside Public Accounting 2023. Internationally, the Firm has offices in Bangalore and Haryana in India and the Ivory Coast in Africa

Think you have what it takes?

Join a vibrant and growing Business Valuation practice at one of South Florida's Best Places to Work. Kaufman Rossin is seeking an experienced Business Valuation professional to join our India team. This role is designed for a self-sufficient professional with direct, hands-on valuation experience who can operate independently with minimal oversight and training.

This position works closely with U.S.-based valuation teams and supports complex valuation engagements involving privately held businesses, intangible assets, and equity-related instruments for financial reporting, tax, transaction, and litigation purposes.

This is not an entry-level or training role. The successful candidate will be expected to contribute immediately and take ownership of workstreams.

How You'll Contribute:


Independently perform valuation analyses with limited supervision
Analyze financial statements and normalize earnings
Develop and review valuation models (income, market, and asset-based approaches)
Conduct industry, company, and economic research
Prepare high-quality valuation workpapers and draft report sections suitable for U.S. client delivery
Coordinate directly with U.S.-based Managers and Partners
Identify valuation issues, apply professional judgment, and escalate matters appropriately



Requirements


What Skills You'll Bring:


Direct experience performing business valuations (required)
4-7+ years of relevant valuation experience
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Accounting or Finance
CPA, CFA, or progress toward valuation credentials preferred
Strong financial modeling and analytical skills
Advanced Excel proficiency; ability to work independently within existing templates
Strong written communication skills and comfort working with U.S.-based teams



How You'll Stand Out:


Prior experience working with U.S. valuation firms or Big 4 / national accounting firms
Exposure to valuations for financial reporting, tax, or transaction purposes
Ability to manage multiple engagements with minimal supervision
Professional maturity, sound judgment, and attention to detail



Benefits


Work-Life Balance
People First Company
Hybrid work policy
High level of autonomy and responsibility
Direct exposure to complex U.S.-based valuation engagements
Clear expectations and limited onboarding ramp-up
Collaborative, respectful culture with direct access to senior professionals


We embrace authenticity. Kaufman Rossin is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to creating a culture that reflects the diversity of our firm and clients. We encourage understanding, acceptance, and celebration among all people. That includes lifestyle, education, experience, race, ethnicity, age, religion, physical ability, sexual orientation, and gender identity. Differences make unique varieties.

title: Company Secretary
description:
This role is for one of the Weekday's clients

Salary range: Rs 200000 - Rs 800000 (ie INR 2-8 LPA)

Min Experience: 0 years

Location: Bengaluru

JobType: full-time

Role Summary

We are seeking a Company Secretary professional (Qualified / Semi-Qualified / Fresher) to assist with corporate governance, statutory compliance, and regulatory obligations of the organization. This role includes helping to maintain legal records, ensuring adherence to relevant laws, and providing support to management on corporate affairs.

Requirements

Key Responsibilities

Ensure adherence to the provisions of the Companies Act and other applicable regulations

Maintain statutory registers, records, and submissions

Assist in the preparation and filing of ROC returns, annual filings, and other compliance documents

Support the organization of Board Meetings, including preparation of agendas, minutes, and resolutions

Coordinate with auditors, legal advisors, and regulatory authorities

Monitor legislative changes relevant to the organization and ensure their timely incorporation

Assist in drafting agreements, policies, and internal compliance documentation

Maintain accurate documentation and corporate records

Role-Based Scope

For Qualified Company Secretary (ACS / Member of ICSI):

Manage corporate compliance and secretarial functions independently

Provide counsel to management regarding legal and governance issues

Ensure comprehensive statutory compliance and effectively mitigate risks

For Semi-Qualified Candidates:

Support compliance filers and documentation processes

Assist in preparing for board meetings and submitting regulatory documents

Operate under the guidance of senior management or external Company Secretary

For Freshers (CS Aspirants / Graduates):

Help with documentation, data entry, and monitoring compliance activities

Gain knowledge of ROC filings, statutory registers, and secretarial practices

Assist senior team members with daily compliance tasks

Qualifications

Qualified CS (Member of ICSI) / Semi-Qualified CS / CS Aspirant

Graduates (B.Com / BBA / related fields) are encouraged to apply (for fresher positions)

Skills Required

Fundamental understanding of the Companies Act and corporate laws

Strong attention to detail and accuracy in documentation

Effective communication and coordination skills

Capability to handle confidential information appropriately

Enthusiasm to learn and develop expertise in compliance

Experience

Qualified CS: 2-5+ years preferred

Semi-Qualified: 0-3 years

Freshers: Entry-level or internship experience preferred

Key Attributes

High integrity and professionalism

Organized and process-focused approach

Ability to meet deadlines and adhere to compliance schedules
